reasoning: While programmatic signals indicate high content retention (0.958 recall), the LLM fidelity evaluation identifies significant structural failures that compromise the document's accessibility and utility. Specifically, the collapse of row structures in the Roll Call table and the data misalignment in the GUESTS table represent major structural defects that fall below the threshold for automatic approval.

concerns.analysis: Multiple structural failures occur in the table processing. First, the 'NORTH CAROLINA 911 BOARD MEETING ROLL CALL' table on page 2 has its row structure collapsed; the names of members and staff are merged into single multi-line cells instead of being partitioned into individual rows as in the source. Second, the 'NORTH CAROLINA 911 BOARD MEETING ROLL CALL VOTE' table appearing on pages 4 and 6 is missing as a table structure entirely, having been captured only as an image placeholder.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The ROLL CALL table on page 2 contains rows for each member (Bone, David; Boyette, Eric, etc.). The ROLL CALL VOTE table appears as a data table on pages 4 and 6.
concerns.output_quote: The element at index 4 ('NORTH CAROLINA 911 BOARD MEETING ROLL CALL') has a single data row where column 1 text is: "Bone, David\nBoyette, Eric..." and column 3 is: "X\nX...". The element at index 12 is an image with alt text "Blank or solid-color image" instead of the vote table.

concerns.analysis: The 'GUESTS' table from page 3 of the source PDF is misrendered in the HTML. In the PDF, this is a clear table with individual rows for each guest name and their corresponding attendance status ('X'). In the HTML rendering, the names are concatenated into large blocks of text within a single cell, and the 'X' marks are similarly grouped in an adjacent cell. This breaks the visual alignment between the rows, making it impossible for a sighted user to read and associate the attendance marks with t
concerns.source_pdf_quote: In the source PDF, page 3 shows a 'GUESTS' table with clear row-by-row alignment between names (e.g., 'Adams, Ron') and attendance status ('X').
concerns.output_quote: In the HTML screenshot, the 'GUESTS' table displays names as a single block of text ('GUESTS ADAMS, Ron Albertson, Michael...') and a corresponding block of 'X' characters, losing the discrete row structure.
concerns.location: GUESTS table, following the ROLL CALL section

concerns.analysis: The list structure is fragmented. Item 6 (Executive Director Report) is split across two separate elements, with sub-items a and b in a list item and items c through g in a following paragraph. Additionally, nested sub-lists (e.g., 5a-f, 8a) are flattened into long text strings within their parent list items rather than being preserved as structured nested lists.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Item 6 on page 4 includes sub-items a through g. Item 5 on page 3 includes sub-items a and b.
concerns.output_quote: List element at index 14 only contains 6a-b. Sub-items are flattened, e.g., 'a) Minutes of Previous Meeting... b) NG 911 Fund/Account...'

action_items: Manually reconstruct the Roll Call and Guests tables to ensure correct row/column partitioning and data association.
Verify the Roll Call Vote table content and convert from image placeholder to accessible HTML table.
Fix nested list fragmentation to ensure logical reading order and proper nesting levels.
